_meta.author: Claude (national autopilot — federal tier, commit_refinement.py validation)
_meta.note: VA US House incumbents. Wittman (R-01): strong social-conservative but America-First deviations (Ukraine aid YES, AIPAC #2 donor → foreign_policy FALSE; 21% defense-industry money + no clean-audit → industry FALSE). Scott (D-03): uniform progressive; credited one point for criticizing the unauthorized Iran strike on Article I grounds.

profile.religion: Episcopalian — St. James Episcopal Church, Montross VA
profile.background: U.S. House VA-01 since 2007. Armed Services Committee Vice Chair (Tactical Air & Land Forces chair; former Seapower chair). Former environmental health specialist; PhD public policy. Heritage Action lifetime 74%.

rob-wittman.notes_set: U.S. Rep., VA-01 (R), since 2007; Armed Services Vice Chair. [2026-06 national refinement, federal tier] Strong on social/governance: pro-life (Born-Alive cosponsor, SBA 'A'); NAY on Respect for Marriage Act; Parents Bill of Rights; SAVE Act YES; HR2 Secure the Border YES; NRA 'A'; FY23 NDAA repealed the military COVID vaccine mandate. AMERICA-FIRST DEVIATIONS (docked): voted YES on ~$60B Ukraine aid and is a vocal pro-Israel hawk with AIPAC as his #2 organizational donor (foreign_policy FALSE), and ~21% of 2024 funds came from the defense sector he oversees with no recorded clean-Pentagon-audit vote (industry_capture FALSE). Heritage Action lifetime 74% (current session softer at 64%).

0.note: Original cosponsor of the Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act; SBA Pro-Life 'A' rating.

1.note: Voted to defund Planned Parenthood and make the Hyde Amendment permanent.

0.note: Voted NAY on the Respect for Marriage Act (both passage and concurrence).

1.note: Opposed federal codification of same-sex marriage (RFMA NAY).

4.note: Opposed federal promotion of SOGI policy.

0.note: Voted for the Parents Bill of Rights Act; endorsed by national school-choice advocates.

1.note: Parents Bill of Rights — parental notification/transparency.

2.note: Parents Bill of Rights — curriculum transparency.
Voted for the Limit, Save, Grow Act (spending caps); cosponsors a debt-limit supermajority amendment.

1.note: Voted YES on the SAVE Act (documentary proof of citizenship to register).

0.note: Voted YES on H.R.2 Secure the Border Act (wall + enforcement).

1.note: NRA-PVF 'A' rating + endorsement; opposes new gun bans.
Voted YES on ~$60B Ukraine aid as a vocal Republican backer (interventionist, not restraint).

3.note: AIPAC was his #2 organizational contributor in 2024 (foreign-lobby money).

0.note: Backed the FY23 NDAA that repealed the military COVID vaccine mandate.

4.note: ~21% of 2024 funds from the defense sector he oversees as Armed Services Vice Chair; no recorded clean-Pentagon-audit vote, topline pro-spending.

profile.religion: Episcopalian — St. Augustine's Episcopal Church, Newport News
profile.background: U.S. House VA-03 since 1993; Ranking Member, Education & Workforce. Attorney. First African-American elected to Congress from Virginia since Reconstruction. Heritage Action lifetime 8%.

bobby-scott.notes_set: U.S. Rep., VA-03 (D), since 1993; Ranking Member of Education & Workforce. [2026-06 national refinement, federal tier] Uniform progressive (Heritage Action 8% lifetime / 0% current): pro-abortion-rights (WHPA cosponsor; NARAL 100%, NRLC 0%); RFMA YES + Equality Act sponsor (LGBTQ Equality Caucus); leads House Democrats against school choice; Christian Coalition 8%, NO on school prayer; Raise the Wage sponsor; HR1 supporter; NO on H.R.2; NRA 'F' + Gun Violence Prevention Task Force vice chair. ONE conservative-aligned note (credited): criticized the unauthorized strike on Iran as a violation of Article I war powers.

0.note: Cosponsored/voted the Women's Health Protection Act ('enshrine Roe'); NARAL 100%, NRLC 0%.

1.note: Voted against partial-birth-abortion ban and abortion restrictions.

0.note: Voted YES on the Respect for Marriage Act; House LGBTQ Equality Caucus member.
Lead House Democrat against school choice/vouchers/scholarship tax credits.

1.note: Sponsored the Equality Act, which explicitly limits RFRA conscience defenses.

3.note: Voted NO on permitting school prayer; ACLU 100%, Christian Coalition 8% (strict separationist).

2.note: Chief sponsor of the Raise the Wage Act ($15 min wage); voted against tax cuts; AFL-CIO 100%.

1.note: Supported HR1 / For the People Act; no support for photo voter ID.

0.note: Voted NO on H.R.2 Secure the Border Act.

1.note: Opposes ICE enforcement expansion; cosponsored the Dream and Promise Act.
NRA 'F'; vice chair of the House Gun Violence Prevention Task Force.

0.note: Criticized the strike on Iran as unconstitutional — no congressional authorization, no imminent threat (Article I war-powers position).
